message:
feat(c-api): add SQLite C API compatibility shim crate

Add fsqlite-c-api, a new crate providing a drop-in C FFI replacement for
the core SQLite C API functions. This is the adoption wedge enabling
existing C/C++ applications to link against frankensqlite without source
changes.

Implemented API surface:
- sqlite3_open / sqlite3_open_v2 / sqlite3_close / sqlite3_close_v2
- sqlite3_exec (with callback support)
- sqlite3_prepare_v2 / sqlite3_step / sqlite3_finalize / sqlite3_reset
- sqlite3_column_count / sqlite3_column_type / sqlite3_column_name
- sqlite3_column_int / sqlite3_column_int64 / sqlite3_column_double
- sqlite3_column_text / sqlite3_column_bytes / sqlite3_column_blob
- sqlite3_bind_int / sqlite3_bind_int64 / sqlite3_bind_double
- sqlite3_bind_text / sqlite3_bind_blob / sqlite3_bind_null
- sqlite3_bind_parameter_count
- sqlite3_errmsg / sqlite3_errcode
- sqlite3_changes / sqlite3_last_insert_rowid
- sqlite3_libversion / sqlite3_libversion_number

Includes per-API-function call counters with CompatMetricsSnapshot for
observability and diagnostics.

Builds as cdylib, staticlib, and rlib to support all linking modes.
Allows unsafe_code only in this crate (required for FFI boundary).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
